description: DALP for Estonia's Digital Assets Market: regulatory alignment, platform capabilities, and MiCA compliance for the Estonian market.

slides.title: DALP for Estonia's Digital Assets Market
slides.subtitle: Regulated digital asset operations for the Baltic region

slides.title: Estonia Market Context
slides.subtitle: A digital-first economy ready for regulated tokenization

slides.title: Estonia's Regulatory Landscape
slides.body:
Regulatory Foundation

• Estonia was among the first EU states to issue VASP licenses, establishing early credibility in digital asset regulation.
• Finantsinspektsioon (FI) now oversees virtual asset service providers under tightened 2022 rules.
• Full MiCA alignment is underway, replacing national VASP frameworks with harmonized EU standards.
• The e-Residency program demonstrates Estonia's commitment to digital-first governance.
slides.body_right:
MiCA Transition

• Existing VASP license holders must transition to MiCA-compliant authorization by end of 2025.
• MiCA introduces standardized requirements for asset-referenced tokens, e-money tokens, and crypto-asset service providers.
• Estonia's digital infrastructure gives it a head start: e-ID, X-Road interoperability, and a mature fintech ecosystem.
• DALP aligns directly with MiCA's compliance, reporting, and governance requirements.

slides.title: Market Opportunity in Estonia
slides.body:
• Estonia hosts 400+ fintech companies and 30+ licensed VASPs, making it one of the densest digital asset markets in Europe per capita.
• The Baltic bond market is growing, with Nasdaq Baltic providing a regional listing venue for fixed income instruments.
• Cross-border capital flows between Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania create demand for compliant digital transfer mechanisms.
• Estonian institutions are exploring tokenized deposits and stablecoins as alternatives to traditional settlement.
• The e-Residency program attracts global entrepreneurs who need compliant digital asset infrastructure.

slides.title: DALP Platform
slides.subtitle: Full lifecycle management for digital assets

slides.title: DALP Platform Overview
slides.body:
• DALP covers the full digital asset lifecycle: design, issuance, compliance, servicing, and operations.
• Seven asset classes supported out of the box: bonds, equities, funds, stablecoins, deposits, precious metals, and real estate.
• Compliance is built into the asset configuration, not bolted on after deployment.
• Maker-checker approval workflows, RBAC/ABAC access controls, and full audit trails are native to every operation.
• White-label deployment allows institutions to present DALP under their own brand.

slides.title: Operational Dashboard
slides.body: The DALP dashboard provides a real-time command center: portfolio valuation, asset tracking, pending action alerts, and a live blockchain activity feed. Operators see their entire digital asset portfolio, identity verifications, and compliance status in one view.

slides.title: Asset Classes for the Estonian Market
slides.col1:
Fixed Income
• Tokenize Baltic bonds with automated yield schedules and maturity redemption.
• ISIN support for Nasdaq Baltic listed instruments.
• Multi-currency denomination across EUR and regional currencies.
• Full coupon lifecycle managed on-chain.
slides.col2:
Cash Equivalents
• Issue EUR-pegged stablecoins compliant with MiCA e-money token rules.
• Tokenize bank deposits for institutional cash management.
• 1:1 fiat peg with on-chain collateral tracking.
• Rolling supply caps aligned to MiCA limits.
slides.col3:
Equity and Funds
• Tokenize common, preferred, and voting shares with transfer restrictions.
• Fund tokenization with fractional ownership at 18-decimal precision.
• Collateral coverage tracking and investor count controls.
• Configurable compliance per share class.

slides.title: Asset Designer: Step-by-Step Tokenization
slides.body: DALP's Asset Designer guides issuers through tokenization with a step-by-step wizard. Each asset is configured with its compliance modules, pricing, permissions, and governance before deployment. The same wizard supports all seven asset classes.

slides.title: Compliance and MiCA
slides.subtitle: Regulatory controls built into every asset

slides.title: MiCA Compliance in DALP
slides.body:
Built-in Controls

• Pre-built MiCA EU Standard compliance template with SMART identity verification, country allowlists, and rolling supply caps.
• 8,000,000 EUR per 12-month rolling window supply cap for e-money tokens, enforced at the smart contract level.
• Investor count limits, identity allowlists/blocklists, and transfer approval workflows.
• Every compliance control change is permanently logged on-chain.
slides.body_right:
Operational Governance

• Maker-checker workflows ensure no single operator can execute critical actions alone.
• Role-based access control separates admin, issuer, custodian, and operator permissions.
• On-chain identity contracts (ONCHAINID) provide cryptographic attestation for KYC, AML, and investor classification.
• Custom compliance templates can be built for Estonian-specific requirements alongside MiCA standards.

slides.title: Compliance Policy Templates
slides.body: DALP ships with pre-built compliance templates for MiCA EU, Japan FSA, MAS Singapore, SEC Reg D, and UK FCA. Each template bundles the controls required by that regulatory framework. Custom templates can be created for Estonian-specific rules.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/jpg/Compliance Policy Templates.jpg

slides.title: Identity and Verification
slides.body:
• Every platform participant has an on-chain identity contract with cryptographic verifications for KYC, AML, and investor classification.
• DALP supports integration with Estonian e-ID and Smart-ID for seamless investor onboarding.
• Verification topics are configurable: system defaults cover accredited investor, AML, and asset classification, with custom topics for Estonian requirements.
• Trusted issuer registry controls who can issue identity claims, establishing a permissioned trust framework.
• 384+ active verifications managed across the platform with full audit trail.

slides.title: Deployment Model
slides.body:
• DALP deploys on private or consortium blockchains with full infrastructure control.
• Self-hosted or managed deployment options for Estonian institutions.
• Kubernetes-native architecture with automated scaling and high availability.
• Full-stack observability: metrics (VictoriaMetrics), logs (Loki), traces (Tempo), and pre-built Grafana dashboards.
• API-first design with REST endpoints for all platform operations.

slides.title: Integration Capabilities
slides.body: DALP integrates with existing Estonian financial infrastructure through standard APIs and identity protocols. The table below maps key integration points.

slides.title: Infrastructure Monitoring
slides.body: DALP includes built-in monitoring for both blockchain infrastructure and API health. Chain RPC status, indexer sync, request volumes, error rates, and response times are tracked in real time with 24-hour trend visualization.

slides.title: Why SettleMint for Estonia
slides.body:
• DALP is a platform, not a consulting engagement: institutions configure and operate their own digital asset programs.
• MiCA compliance is native, not an afterthought. The same compliance engine covers Estonia's transition from VASP to MiCA.
• Seven asset classes out of the box means institutions can start with bonds and expand to deposits, stablecoins, or equity without re-platforming.
• White-label deployment lets Estonian institutions present the platform under their own brand.
• SettleMint has deep European regulatory experience and active deployments across EU jurisdictions.

slides.title: Appendix
slides.subtitle: Reference diagrams for the Estonian deployment model

slides.title: Estonian Bond Lifecycle
slides.body: End-to-end bond lifecycle from term definition through compliance configuration, smart contract deployment, minting, distribution, yield scheduling, and maturity redemption. Each stage maps to a governed DALP workflow step with on-chain audit evidence.

slides.title: MiCA Compliance Flow
slides.body: The MiCA compliance flow shows how investor onboarding, KYC verification, AML checks, country allowlisting, and investor classification work together to gate access and transfer eligibility. Policy evaluation happens at every transfer request before blockchain execution.

slides.title: Estonia Deployment Roadmap
slides.body: Implementation timeline from discovery through configuration, integration, validation, and launch. Covers regulatory mapping, compliance policy setup, core banking integration, and FI submission milestones.

slides.title: DALP Platform Topology
slides.body: Platform architecture showing the relationship between access channels (web portal, API, SDK), DALP core services (asset designer, compliance engine, identity manager, operations hub), blockchain infrastructure, and external integration points.

slides.title: Estonian Integration Model
slides.body: Integration architecture connecting DALP with Estonian market infrastructure: e-ID for identity verification, Finantsinspektsioon for regulatory reporting, Baltic CSD for settlement bridging, and local custodians for asset safekeeping. The model supports both domestic operations and cross-border Baltic transfers.
